title: Introduction to Python & Data Science
description: Build Python fluency from scratch using real financial datasets — variables, functions, pandas DataFrames, and data cleaning workflows applied to FinTech business problems.
lesson_titles: Python Basics: Variables, Types & Your First Script
Control Flow: Conditionals, Loops & Business Logic
Functions: Writing Reusable Financial Calculations
Pandas Part I: Series, DataFrames & Indexing
Pandas Part II: Filtering, Sorting & Slicing Data
Data Cleaning: Handling Missing Values & Outliers
GroupBy & Aggregation: Summarizing Financial Data
Putting It Together: End-to-End EDA on a Loan Dataset

title: Database Design & SQL
description: Design relational schemas for financial data and write SQL queries — from basic SELECT statements to window functions — to answer real business questions.
lesson_titles: Relational Data Models & FinTech Schemas
SELECT Statements: Filtering & Sorting Financial Records
JOINs: Combining Transactions, Users & Accounts
Aggregations: GROUP BY, HAVING & Summary Statistics
Subqueries & CTEs: Writing Readable Complex Queries
Window Functions: Running Totals, Ranks & Rolling Averages

title: Data Visualization for Business Insights
description: Turn data into decisions using matplotlib, seaborn, and Plotly — from exploratory charts to polished executive dashboards that communicate financial insight clearly.
lesson_titles: Communicating with Data: Charts That Drive Decisions
Matplotlib Foundations: Lines, Bars & Scatter Plots
Seaborn: Statistical Visualizations for Financial Distributions
Interactive Charts with Plotly: Drill-Downs & Hover Data
Dashboard Design: Layout, Color & Annotation Principles
Capstone: Building a Portfolio Performance Dashboard

title: Machine Learning for Business Outcomes
description: Apply supervised and unsupervised machine learning to real finance and business problems — credit default, churn prediction, and customer segmentation — with scikit-learn.
lesson_titles: Supervised Learning: Predicting Credit Default
Linear Regression: Modeling Continuous Financial Outcomes
Logistic Regression: Classification & Probability Scores
Decision Trees & Random Forests: Ensemble Methods
Feature Engineering: Creating Predictive Variables from Raw Data
Model Evaluation: AUC, Precision, Recall & Business Cost
Cross-Validation & Hyperparameter Tuning
Unsupervised Learning: Clustering for Customer Segmentation

title: Text Mining & Natural Language Processing
description: Extract signal from unstructured financial text — earnings call transcripts, news articles, and SEC filings — using NLP techniques including sentiment analysis and topic modeling.
lesson_titles: Extracting Signal from Financial News
Text Preprocessing: Tokenization, Stopwords & Stemming
TF-IDF & Bag of Words: Vectorizing Financial Documents
Sentiment Analysis: Reading the Market's Mood
Topic Modeling & Named Entity Recognition for Earnings Calls

title: Prescriptive Analytics & Optimization
description: Go beyond prediction to prescription — use linear programming and optimization techniques to allocate capital, minimize risk, and make data-driven portfolio decisions.
lesson_titles: Optimization: Allocating Capital Across Investments
Linear Programming: Formulating Business Constraints
Solving Optimization Problems with scipy
Mean-Variance Portfolio Optimization
Scenario Analysis & Sensitivity in Optimization Models

title: A/B Testing in Business & Social Science
description: Design, run, and interpret experiments that drive product and business decisions — from conversion rate optimization to policy evaluation using causal inference methods.
lesson_titles: Designing Experiments: Conversion Rate Optimization
Hypothesis Testing: Null Hypotheses & p-Values
Power Analysis: Sample Size & Detecting Real Effects
Chi-Square & t-Tests: Choosing the Right Test
Causal Inference: Beyond Correlation in Business Decisions

title: Big Data & Cloud Computing
description: Scale your analytics beyond a single machine — understand distributed computing frameworks, cloud data warehouses, and when big data infrastructure is and isn't the right tool.
lesson_titles: Scale: When pandas Isn't Enough
Apache Spark: Distributed DataFrames for Large Datasets
Cloud Data Warehouses: Snowflake, BigQuery & Redshift
ETL Pipelines: Moving and Transforming Financial Data
Data Lakes & Modern Data Architecture

title: Financial Markets & Instruments
description: Build fluency across asset classes — equities, fixed income, derivatives, and alternatives — and understand how market microstructure shapes pricing and liquidity.
lesson_titles: Asset Classes: Equities, Fixed Income & Derivatives
Equity Valuation: P/E, DCF & Comparable Companies
Fixed Income: Bond Pricing, Duration & Yield Curves
Options & Futures: Pricing, Payoffs & Hedging
Market Microstructure: Order Books, Liquidity & Spreads
Risk Metrics: Beta, VaR & Sharpe Ratio in Python

title: Corporate Finance & Capital Structure
description: Understand how firms make capital allocation decisions — NPV, IRR, WACC, and optimal capital structure — and apply financial modeling techniques to real corporate scenarios.
lesson_titles: Capital Structure: Debt, Equity & the Optimal Mix
NPV & IRR: Evaluating Investment Decisions
WACC: Cost of Capital Across the Capital Stack
DCF Modeling: Building a Three-Statement Forecast
Financial Statement Analysis: Reading the Numbers That Matter

title: Mergers & Acquisitions
description: Understand how M&A deals are structured, valued, and executed — from deal origination and due diligence through synergy analysis and post-merger integration.
lesson_titles: Deal Structure: Asset vs. Stock Acquisitions
Valuation in M&A: Precedent Transactions & Control Premiums
Synergy Analysis: Revenue, Cost & Financial Synergies
Due Diligence: Legal, Financial & Operational Review
Accretion/Dilution Analysis & Post-Merger Integration

title: Data Analysis, Forecasting & Risk
description: Apply data science to credit risk, default modeling, and lending decisions — from scoring models and threshold optimization to fair lending compliance and policy design.
lesson_titles: Credit Risk Fundamentals: What Drives Default
Building a Credit Scorecard: Features & Logistic Regression
Threshold Optimization: Balancing Approval Rate & Default Risk
Expected Value Framework: Pricing Risk into Lending Decisions
Fair Lending & Disparate Impact: Compliance Analytics
Stress Testing: Portfolio Risk Under Adverse Scenarios

title: Venture Capital — Methods
description: Master the analytical foundations of venture investing — fund economics, term sheet mechanics, valuation methods, and the power law math that drives VC portfolio construction.
lesson_titles: The VC Model: Fund Structure, LPs & Return Mechanics
Term Sheets: Valuation, Equity & Protective Provisions
Due Diligence: Market Sizing, Team Assessment & Financials
Portfolio Theory: Power Law Returns & Fund Construction
Post-Investment: Board Seats, Milestones & Follow-On
Exit Strategies: IPO, M&A & Secondary Sales

title: Venture Capital — Practice
description: Apply VC methods to real deal practice — sourcing pipeline management, pitch deck evaluation, founder meetings, portfolio monitoring, and building a data-driven investment thesis.
lesson_titles: Sourcing & Screening: Deal Flow & First Meetings
Evaluating Pitches: Framework for Assessing Startup Potential
Founder Diligence: Reference Checks & Team Assessment
Writing Investment Memos: Structure, Thesis & Risk Factors
Portfolio Monitoring: KPIs, Board Meetings & Reserves
Fund Reporting: LP Updates, TVPI, DPI & RVPI

title: Private Equity — Methods
description: Build the technical foundation of private equity — LBO mechanics, returns attribution, debt structuring, and financial modeling techniques used in buyout analysis.
lesson_titles: Leveraged Buyouts: Structure, Returns & Value Creation
LBO Modeling: Building a Buyout Model from Scratch
Debt Structuring: Senior, Mezzanine & Equity Tranches
Returns Attribution: Multiple Expansion, Leverage & EBITDA Growth
Operating Improvements: Margin Expansion & Revenue Initiatives
Exit Analysis: Timing, Multiples & IRR Sensitivity

title: Private Equity — Practice
description: Apply PE methods through the full deal lifecycle — sourcing, financial modeling, management presentations, 100-day plans, and preparing portfolio companies for exit.
lesson_titles: Due Diligence: Financial Modeling & Red Flags
Management Presentations: What PE Firms Look For
Operational Due Diligence: Business Quality Assessment
100-Day Plans: Value Creation from Day One
Portfolio Operations: Working with Management Teams
Preparing for Exit: Clean-Up, Positioning & the Sale Process

title: Deep Learning for Business Prediction
description: Understand and apply neural networks and deep learning to high-value financial prediction problems — from time series forecasting to fraud detection at scale.
lesson_titles: Neural Networks: From Perceptrons to Business Prediction
Building Your First Neural Network with Keras
Recurrent Networks & LSTMs: Modeling Sequential Financial Data
Time Series Forecasting: Stock Prices & Revenue Projection
Fraud Detection at Scale: Deep Learning for Anomaly Detection
Deploying Models: From Jupyter Notebook to Production API

title: Blockchain & Cryptocurrency Analytics
description: Analyze blockchain data, understand DeFi protocols, and apply quantitative methods to cryptocurrency markets — bridging distributed ledger technology with financial analytics.
lesson_titles: Distributed Ledgers: How Blockchain Works in Finance
Smart Contracts: Programmable Finance & DeFi Protocols
On-Chain Analytics: Querying Blockchain Data with Python
Crypto Market Structure: Exchanges, Liquidity & Volatility
Tokenization: Real-World Assets on the Blockchain

title: Introduction to Cybersecurity
description: Understand the cybersecurity threat landscape facing financial institutions — attack vectors, regulatory frameworks, and how analytics is used to detect and respond to breaches.
lesson_titles: Threat Landscape: FinTech Attack Vectors & Defenses
Identity & Authentication: Fraud Prevention Systems
Regulatory Frameworks: SOC 2, GDPR & Financial Compliance
Security Analytics: Detecting Anomalies & Insider Threats

title: Investment Praxis
description: Bridge investment theory and live markets — portfolio construction, factor exposure analysis, performance attribution, and executing a data-driven investment process.
lesson_titles: Portfolio Construction: Theory Meets Practice
Factor Investing: Value, Momentum & Quality Premia
Performance Attribution: What Drove Returns?
Risk Budgeting: Sizing Positions by Risk Contribution

title: International Finance
description: Navigate currency risk, cross-border capital flows, and international capital markets — essential for finance roles with global portfolio or operational exposure.
lesson_titles: FX Risk: Currency Exposure in Global Portfolios
Hedging Currency Risk: Forwards, Options & Natural Hedges
International Capital Markets: Cross-Border Investing
Emerging Markets: Risk Premium, Contagion & Opportunity

title: Wealth Management Practice
description: Apply quantitative and behavioral finance tools to high-net-worth client advisory — financial planning, asset allocation, tax-aware investing, and practice management.
lesson_titles: Client Goals: Risk Tolerance & Financial Planning
Asset Allocation: Building Model Portfolios for Client Profiles
Tax-Aware Investing: Loss Harvesting & Asset Location
Practice Management: Technology & Analytics in Wealth Advising
